response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack developer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE, plus a bachelor's thesis project—nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on C++ Windows development required. He has no demonstrated experience in capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking systems; his only financial-sector employer (RIDE Capital) used a Node.js/TypeScript stack for retail investment tooling. SQL appears in his skills list but without any evidence of depth in SQL Server or Oracle, and there is no mention of Summit, Kafka, or relevant financial messaging infrastructure.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases)
Solid CI/CD and DevOps background with GitLab, Docker, trunk-based development, and artifact management across multiple roles
Java background and some C/C++ exposure from National Instruments and embedded/hardware projects at Altom Consulting
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated—approximately six months of professional use in 2014–2015, far below the mandatory 5–10 year threshold
No capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge whatsoever; financial-sector exposure is limited to a Node.js startup focused on retail investment workflows
No evidence of Windows platform development, SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, Summit familiarity, or financial messaging frameworks such as Kafka or MQ
